note: All recipe (response-graded) — no AI provider/API key needed, so these run in CI without mutating the site or spending on an LLM vendor. Each probes an ai_automators architecture fact an unfamiliar agent would otherwise have to read src/ to answer.

evals.prompt: The Drupal ai_automators module defines two plugin types: one for the field-generation 'rule' and one for the 'worker type' (how/when processing runs). Name both plugin types and their Plugin/ namespaces.
evals.expected_output: The rule plugin type is AiAutomatorType (namespace Plugin/AiAutomatorType, manager plugin.manager.ai_automator); the worker/process type is AiAutomatorProcessRule (namespace Plugin/AiAutomatorProcess, manager plugin.manager.ai_processor).

evals.prompt: In ai_automators I want to write a rule that auto-generates a value for a custom field type. Which attribute do I put on the class, which attribute property ties it to a field type, and where does the class live?
evals.expected_output: Add #[AiAutomatorType] to a class under src/Plugin/AiAutomatorType/ in your module; set field_rule to the target field type id (e.g. 'string'), extend a base class from src/PluginBaseClasses/ (e.g. ComplexTextChat) and implement AiAutomatorTypeInterface.

evals.prompt: In ai_automators, what are the shipped 'worker type' processing options that control how/when an automator runs on a field (e.g. immediately vs deferred)?
evals.expected_output: The AiAutomatorProcessRule plugins in Plugin/AiAutomatorProcess/: DirectSaveProcessing, QueueWorkerProcessor, BatchProcessing, ActionProcessing, FieldWidgetProcessing.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site with ai_automators, how do I attach an automator to a field, and what config entity stores that configuration? Is there a dedicated settings form?
evals.expected_output: There is no dedicated settings form; configure it on the field's own config form in Field UI (the module alters field_config_edit_form). Each attachment is stored as an ai_automator config entity holding rule, worker_type, entity_type, bundle, field_name, prompt, token.

evals.prompt: What drush command does ai_automators provide to scaffold a new automator plugin, and is it a runtime command or a code generator?
evals.expected_output: It is a code generator, not a runtime command: drush generate plugin:ai:automator-type (alias ai-automator-type), which scaffolds an AiAutomatorType plugin under src/Plugin/AiAutomatorType/.

evals.prompt: In ai_automators, which service lets me run a reusable automator chain from custom code and get back the generated field values, and what method do I call?
evals.expected_output: The ai_automator.automate service (Drupal\ai_automators\Service\Automate); call ->run($chain_type, $inputs), which creates a disposable automator_chain, runs it, returns [output_field => value] and deletes the temp entity.

evals.prompt: In ai_automators, I want to override whether a given field should be processed by an automator, without editing the rule. There is no classic hook API for this — what mechanism and which event do I use?
evals.expected_output: Use a Symfony event subscriber on ShouldProcessFieldEvent (EVENT_NAME 'ai_automator.should_process_field') and call setShouldProcess(TRUE/FALSE). Other events: ValuesChangeEvent (ai_automator.change_value), ProcessFieldEvent, RuleIsAllowedEvent, VerboseInformationEvent.

evals.prompt: In ai_automators, which permission machine name gates configuring automators (the AI Automators area and the automator section on field forms)?
